text:
Once upon a time in the verdant forest of Eldergrove lived a curious squirrel named Holly. Holly loved exploring the ancient trees that lined the forest path. One particular trunk had a hole perfect for a small chair. Every afternoon she’d swoon into that chair and drift away on dreams of acorns. One day, a wise old owl perched there listened. Holly told her tales of adventure across the meadow.

“Miss Holly,” the owl said thoughtfully. “Would you like to explore the world?”

“You have no idea how I’d love that!”

“Then I’ve found the perfect sanctuary for you.”

She’d open a book full of stories about different landscapes.

Holly lived there for seasons. She’d talk with the owl about adventures—like visiting a castle made of leaves. She’d dance in rainbows. The owl taught her to holler from branches like a cat.

One autumn, Holly discovered hidden treasure.

She’d find little golden things. Those treasures made her
